title: Column permission in name column
excerpt: I find really usefull the column permission but it is available in all columns except the name. Name is maybe the most important column, i think it should be possible to also set edition permission in this column. Is the…

posts.author: elena (elena)
posts.cookedHtml: I find really usefull the column permission but it is available in all columns except the name. Name is maybe the most important column, i think it should be possible to also set edition permission in this column. Is there a way to do that?

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: This really is a good one feature request, at least the edition permission (in some workflows) needs to be set.

posts.author: pjvoysey (PJ Voysey)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. I also would like to be able to restrict editing of the item/subitem name.

posts.author: DavidSchenkler (David Schenkler | monday ambassador cohort 7)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d really like this feature to exist! So important. If it is impossible to get rid of the item/subitem, then to potentially gray it out so that you only see the actual information needed.

For example, I need very high-level finance people to see the projected and approved costs, but I don’t need them to see where they are going. The only reason they need to see it is for their own boards where this information is MIRRORED from the source board.
